title: code-flow flow.json
description: Canonical artifact produced by the code-flow skill. A structured walk of a code path that downstream consumers (docs, viewers, incident postmortems) can enrich and render.

schemaVersion.description: Semver string for the flow.json schema. Bump when the schema gains/loses fields. Current: 1.0.0.

name.description: Filesystem-safe short identifier for the flow (used as the artifact's basename).

description.description: Optional one-sentence summary of what this flow covers.

mode.description: base = pure code-walk; incident = code-walk grounded in a specific failed run's logs.

incident.description: Present only when mode == 'incident'. Captures the specific failed run being investigated.

ids.description: Typed identifiers that locate the run in logs. Type strings are free-form (e.g. RootActivityId, requestId, traceId, workspaceId).

url.description: Optional URL of the failing request, useful when the flow models an HTTP path.

description.description: Optional free-text from the user. Anything incident-related that isn't an ID — symptoms, scenario, environment, repro notes. Quoted verbatim; not paraphrased.

rootCause.description: Causal chain explaining why the run failed: what triggered it, why it failed, cascading effects. Required in incident mode.

step.description: Monotonically increasing integer starting at 1. Never reset, never skip.

kind.description: failure-point = throws/branches/cancels/retries; call-step = everything else worth noting.

title.description: One-line 'who does what' headline, scannable in a table.

entity.description: Where the step lives in code. Required.

action.description: Two-sentence max prose: what it does, and how it fails (when applicable). No code snippets.

codeLink.description: Optional URL to the source line in a hosted repo (ADO/GitHub/GitLab). Omit for local-only repos.

logAnchor.description: Optional pointer to an observable log/trace line emitted by the step, used by incident mode for status inference.

marker.description: Exact log string (or stable marker/event-id) emitted by the step. Greppable in source.

source.description: Optional URL of the log-emit site (not the log table or query UI).

status.description: Only present in incident mode. Required on every step when present at all.
